text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While heading home with Hishi Akebono after a
day out, we talk about the ceramics workshop
again. She's booked another lesson and can't wait
to attend.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I've been watching loads of videos on how to use
the pottery wheel and looking up different
ceramics tips and tricks, too.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I know watching videos and doing research won't
make me a master, but it'd be nice if I could do
better than last time!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Sir? I designed this pot to match my own
preferences, but are there rules I should follow to
make it better?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Is there a "right way" to do this? You know,
ceramically?

text_block_list.name: Ceramics Instructor
text_block_list.text:
You don't have to think in such black and white
terms. No matter what you're doing, it's probably
best to start by taking an interest in it, then
learning to like it.

text_block_list.name: Ceramics Instructor
text_block_list.text:
There's no right way when it comes to creating
things. You just have to give form to whatever it
is you wish to make.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: There's no right way...

text_block_list.name: Ceramics Instructor
text_block_list.text:
Just so. That's why it's so difficult to find the
answer within yourself. Why, I'm still searching
myself. Haha.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
That's true... There isn't a single "right way" to
season or prepare food. The most important thing
is that it's delicious and filling...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I don't really know how to explain it, but... I think
my guts know what you mean!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Ehehe! ☆ Aaall right! I feel like now I can really
make something to my liking.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: I'm going to make a pot that'll make heavenly chanko! Let's do this!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
When Hishi Akebono faces the pottery wheel
again, her expression is stern but soft, just how
she looks when she's cooking.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Although the end result isn't a one-to-one
realization of her design, the smoothly curved
clay pot she ends up with is still very similar to her
draft.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
It's a little smaller than planned, and the shape's a
bit different... but it looks great! Doesn't it,
Trainer?
choice_data_list:
Yeah! I can't wait to see how it'll look when it's
fired!

Yeah! I can't wait to see how it'll look when it's
fired!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Same! Let's come pick it up together after it's
done! ☆

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
So, actually, after I watched those pottery wheel
videos, I revisited some recipes I wrote up when I
was a kid, and tried cooking those dishes again.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
And so many things bothered me about them.
Like how I didn't include ingredients that
would've made the dishes tastier, or didn't
prepare them so they'd look as appetizing as
possible...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
But back then, my parents never ever told me I
had to do stuff a certain way...
choice_data_list:
They just watched and let you figure it out,
huh?

They just watched and let you figure it out,
huh?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Yep. And it made me realize how supportive they
were of me just doing the best I could!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
There's really no need to overthink things, huh?
And I'm sure that the more I like something, the
more stuff I'll notice when I'm doing it.
choice_data_list: Being a trainer is the same.
Being a trainer is the same.
That's what it means to grow.
That's what it means to grow.
What's your favorite thing right now, then?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
There's really no need to overthink things, huh?
And I'm sure that the more I like something, the
more stuff I'll notice when I'm doing it.
choice_data_list: Being a trainer is the same.
Being a trainer is the same.
That's what it means to grow.
That's what it means to grow.
What's your favorite thing right now, then?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: Is it? Hmm... In what way?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I tell her about how it took all kinds of
experiences for me to come to love my job and
know a lot about it. I also explain that it's
because I love my job that I can pick up on so
much.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Maybe everything is the same in the end, whether
it's cooking or ceramics.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Ohhh, okay! That must mean that the more you
come to like me, the more awesome a trainer
you'll become!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
That means I have to work super hard, so you'll
like me even more! ☆

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
That's not what I meant, but... I guess she's not
entirely wrong?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While I struggle to come up with a good
response, Hishi Akebono smiles and nods happily
at me.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Growth! So, the more we like something, the more
we grow... That's so wonderful!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I'm going to love everything even harder! Like
cooking and sumo wrestling and training and you
and eeeveryone!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
That way, I'll grow and improve and become even
bigger and taller than I am now! ☆

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Hehehe! And I already love all those things more
and more every day anyway, so I'm sure to
improve by leaps and bounds! ♪

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A wide smile lights up Hishi Akebono's whole
face, and I can't help but smile myself as we make
our way back home.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text: Huh? You mean my favorite favorite thing? Umm, gosh, what could it be?!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I like eating food, but I like making it, too! And I
like running... But pottery is fun too, and um...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
I love my dad and my mom and you, and my
friends, too! And...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Ahaha! It's impossible for me to choose just one
thing! Everything and everyone is my favorite,
and I love them all.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
There are many other things I love too. For
example...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I listen to her endless list of likes as the train
home sways us back and forth.
